title: MCLN EX92-Cigarette Diversion

3/6/2024 1 .1 Updated to be applicable across divisions Alex Jones 3/15/2024 2.0 Adding work area equivalency logic, updating system code parameter design Alex Jones 3/27/2024 2.1 Allowing alphanumeric barcodes, c ustom column instead of base for LPN table Alex Jones 4/16/2024 2.2 Tech note that print jobs being held does not affect functionality Alex Jones 5/28/2024 3.0 CR01 Adding configurable CIGU specific barcodes based on allocation type EX 92 – Cigarette Diversion Some McLane site s contain conveyer belt s which could be used either throughout a facility or exclusively i n a cigarette cage. The need is to implement a way for MarkMagic to add a diversion barcode to each oLPN based on the destination location associated with its task . For this purpose, WM is enhanced to populate a custom LPN reference field with the necessary diversion code after an oLPN is created during a wave. The logic to populate the field is exclusively primarily dependent on destination work area(s) associated with the oLPN , which are configurable so that the enhancement can be used across multiple facilities. F or oLPNs directed to unstamped cigarette work area CIGU, barcodes are applied first based on the allocation type passed down on th e order line item . Work area configuration barcode application Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF N Reporting N Other Y LPN table , 1 custom field populated based on system code configuration Assumptions T eammates can easily access and change existing physical barcodes on oLPN labels, no ex ception scenarios will be considered . One diver sion barcode will be applied to each oLPN . All possible diversion barcodes will be alpha numeric . No data verification will be performed during configuration . Diversion barcode determination logic will depend solely on provided work areas or allocation type as defined in system code configuration . No changes will be made to any base WM labels or label templates as part of this extension . Additional oLPN diversion barcode value will not be added to existing UIs . There is only one translation value for any given work area. A work area is not defined as being equivalent to more than one other work area. There is only one barcode per allocation type. Allocation types must be unique. There is only one allocation type per LPN. Where multiple exist, the first will be used and others disregarded. In the event that a single task detail is directed towards both CIGU and another work area, C-92C configuration will be disregarded and barcode value will be applied based on the work area configuration held in C-92B. Platform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Configuration System Code Mockup System code enablement will appear similar to the below and will determine the logic behind which barcodes are applied . First, to accommodate the desire to allow certain work areas to be considered equivalent to others for the purposes of applying a diversion barcode, a system code is set up where each parameter description will contain the work area that might appear on the task detail, and the parameter value contains the value it will be interpreted as for the purposes of the barcode. In the below example, where a CIGP work area exists, it will be treated as though it were CIGU. This will not affect any fields or flows apart from the cus tom EX92 flow and subsequent LPN. REF_FIELD_11 update. To accommodate the desire to allow applied diversion barcodes and their associated work areas to be configurable between environments, the work areas required to apply a certain barcode are held in no particular order in the parameter description with commas used as delineators, and the barcode that should be applied is held in the corresponding parameter value. WM performs no data verification on these fields , they will be implemented exactly as configured. o LPNs directed to work area CIGU have barcodes a pplied based on the allocation type for their order line item . Values configured here take precedence over any barcodes configured in C-92B— i f an allocation type barcode is no t configured, the above work area configuration is used instead. Allocation type is defined in the parameter description and compared against ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ALLOC_TYPE. Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below. Tech note: EX92 functionality is unaffected by whether print jobs are held; reference fields are populated regardless of the wave configuration for Printing: Hold print jobs. System Codes System code configuration below enablement is configurable between environments and can contain up to twenty unique rows. Following is one example of a potential valid system code setup. Code ID Details Parameter Configuration Rec Type Code Type Code ID Short Description Description Frm To Description Validation Type Configured value C 000 000 Global Extension Control Global Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – Global Extension Control is turned ON C 000 EX 92 EX 92 Extension Control EX 9 2 Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – EX 92 Extension Control is turned ON C 92A EQV EQV Equivalency 1 4 CIGP Edit Box Alphanumeric Only CIGU C 92A EQV EQV Equivalency 5 8 SWCH Edit Box Alphanumeric Only CIGH C 92B 92B BRCD Barcodes 1 19 CIGU Edit Box Numeric Only 002 C 92B 92B BRCD Barcodes 20 39 CIGK,CIGM ,CIGL Edit Box Numeric Only 012 C 92C 92C CIGUBCD AllocBrcd 1 9 ABC Edit Box Numeric Only 010 C 92C 92C CIGUBCD AllocBrcd 10 19 def Edit Box Numeric Only 020 Database The LPN table is customized to include the below custom column which is used to store the diversion barcode. TABLE NAME COLUMN NAME Nullable Data Type Constraints LPN REF_FIELD_11 Y VARCHAR ( 25 ) none Purge and Archive All custom fields should be purged and archive d when purge and archive is run on the related LPN table. Custom Configurations System Code Types ' The below system code type configuration for parameters below extension control serve as an example . These will be able to differ between environments and can contain up to twenty unique rows. Rec Type Code Type Code Desc Max Len. Allow Add Allow Delete Allow Parm Maint Allow Desc Mod WHSE/ BU/ Ent Parm Exist Order by C 000 Global Extension Control 4 Y Y Y Y WHSE Y Code Id C 000 EX92 Extension Control 4 Y Y Y Y WHSE Y Code Id C 92A Equivalency 10 Y Y Y Y WHSE Y Code Id C 92B Barcodes 10 Y Y Y Y WHSE Y Code Id C 92C AllocBrcd 10 Y Y Y Y WHSE Y Code Id System Code Parameters The below system code parameter configuration for parameters below enablement serve as an example . These will be configurable between environments and can contain up to twenty unique rows. Rec Type Code Type From Byte To Byte Parm Desc Parameter Validation C 000 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag C 000 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag C 92A 1 4 CIGP Edit Box Alphanumeric Only C 92A 5 8 SWCH Edit Box Alphanumeric Only C 92 B 1 9 CIGU Edit Box Alphanumeric Only C 92B 10 29 CIGU,CIGH Edit Box Numeric Only C 92B 30 59 CIGK,CIGM ,CIGL Edit Box Numeric Only C 92C 1 9 ABC Edit Box Numeric Only C 92C 10 19 def Edit Box Numeric Only Validations Validations The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code ID 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code ID EX9 2 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX 92 is turned off, and no custom EX9 2 processing will occur. Last, as the procedure compares oLPN work areas and allocation types agains t parameters configured in system codes , all system code IDs and system code parameters must be configured exactly correctly. If a nonexistent work area is entered, no changes will be made. If an existing but incorrect work area is entered, the diversion barcode field will be applied to the incorrect oLPNs . If the diversion barcode values are incorrect ly configured in code IDs, all resulting diversion fields will also contain incorrect values. WM will assume all values provided are accurate . Updates D uring waving, after task creation, this stored procedure will be triggered. It will target all created oLPNs and will populate in the custom LPN. REF_FIELD_11 field based on the logic defined in the parameters. Parameter values will be compared against all existing TASK_DTL.CURR_WORK_AREA or ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ALLOC_TYPE values for that oLPN . With EX92 fully enabled, t T he logic flow will be as follows: if LPN. REF_FIELD_11 is null if C- 92A is not null, if TASK_DTL.CURR_WORK_AREA matches parameter description, then use parameter value for the following logic else, use TASK_DTL.CURR_WORK_AREA else, use TASK_DTL.CURR_WORK_AREA if C-92C is not null and the TASK_DTL.CURR_WORK_AREA=’CIGU’ compare ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ALLOC_TYPE values against parameter description where LPN.TC_LPN_ID=TASK_DTL.CARTON_NBR where ORDER_LINE_ITEM. LINE_ITEM_ID = TASK_DTL. LINE_ITEM_ID where LPN. ORDER_ID = ORDER_LINE_ITEM. ORDER_ID if sy stem parameter matches ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ALLOC_TYPE values set LPN.REF_FIELD_11 = corresponding parameter value if else, make no changes and proceed to C-92B updates if C-92B is not null compare distinct TASK_DTL.CURR_WORK_AREA values against parameter description where LPN.TC_LPN_ID=TASK_DTL.CARTON_NBR if system parameter matches distinct TASK_DTL.CURR_WORK_AREA values set LPN. REF_FIELD_11 = corresponding parameter value if else, no updates made if else, no updates made if else, no updates made Technical note: SP to be added at later date . Exceptions Not in scope for this extension. Exclusions Dependencies Not in scope for this extension. Process Flow Diagram Not in scope for this extension. MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. Process Change/Addition Not in scope for this extension. Reporting, Label, or Document Not in scope for this extension. Messages Not in scope for this extension. Custom PIX Not in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Test Cases General Conditions Condition Name Description and expectations Task details contain work areas in reverse order as compared to code configuration Correct barcode should be applied Task details contain work area with no corresponding code configuration No updates should be made Questions and Answers This below table is used to track questions that were asked and answered regarding this enhancement. If the question resulted in any action it is listed against the question. # Asked By Question Answered By Answer Date Added Action Taken
